title: Issues running podman on Jenkins agent running RHEL OS
body:
Hello. We are running a Jenkins agent that is using RHEL 8 and running podman (instead of docker), and we noticed an issue where on the command line, commands such as podman pull node and ```podman image build app --no-cache``` works fine. However, when we run the same exact command through a Jenkins pipeline, we get the following error:
```
Error: error creating build container: error preparing image configuration: error reading image configuration for "containers-storage:[overlay@/var/lib/jenkins/.local/share/containers/storage+/run/user/101/containers:overlay.ignore_chown_errors=true]@ac3dcfe39c7e20f85479935fc56f7b59ce270289fac6de229a3e48bf697e0438": open ~/20287918: no such file or directory
```
We were trying to dig for resources online regarding this specific error, but we couldn't find anything. Is there a way to fix this? Thank you!

comments.body:
Please fill out the issue template. We need to know at least `podman info` for most things.
Also if you a RHEL customer please use the Red Hat support channels instead.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we never heard any feedback, I am closing.  Reopen if you still want us to look at it.

Are you running rootful or rootless?

If rootless did you set the user up as enable-linger?

We should not support RHEL issues upstream unless you you use the latest upstream version.
Please go through the Red Hat support channels, e.g. file a bug against podman on https://bugzilla.redhat.com
Also we need a reproducer, otherwise we can only guess which rarely works.
